annotations.description: Extra annotations for the flower Deployment

labels.description: Extra labels for the flower Deployment

spec.description: Configuration for other configurable fields in the Deployment.spec

extraEnvFrom.description: Extra secretRef or configMapRef for the `envFrom` field in the flower container (templated)

nodeSelector.description: Node labels for flower pods assignment # Ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/assign-pod-node/

podAnnotations.description: Add extra annotations to the Invenio flower pods

podLabels.description: Add extra labels to the Invenio flower pods

podSecurityContext.description: SecurityContext for the flower pod # ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-container

securityContext.description: SecurityContext for the flower container # ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-container

tolerations.description: Tolerations for flower pods assignment # Ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/taint-and-toleration/

digest.description: Invenio image digest in the way sha256:aa.... Please note this parameter, if set, will override the tag

pullPolicy.description: Invenio image pull policy # Defaults to 'Always' if image tag is 'latest', else set to 'IfNotPresent' # ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/images/#pre-pulled-images

pullSecrets.description: Invenio image pull secrets # Secrets must be manually created in the namespace. # ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/pull-image-private-registry/ # e.g: # pullSecrets: #   - name: myRegistryKeySecretName

tag.description: Invenio image tag (immutable tags are recommended). Defaults to .Chart.appVersion

annotations.description: Additional annotations for the Ingress resource. To enable certificate autogeneration, place here your cert-manager annotations. # Use this parameter to set the required annotations for cert-manager, see # ref: https://cert-manager.io/docs/usage/ingress/#supported-annotations # e.g: # annotations: #   kubernetes.io/ingress.class: nginx #   cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: cluster-issuer-name

class.description: IngressClass that will be be used to implement the Ingress (Kubernetes 1.18+) # This is supported in Kubernetes 1.18+ and required if you have more than one IngressClass marked as the default for your cluster. # ref: https://kubernetes.io/blog/2020/04/02/improvements-to-the-ingress-api-in-kubernetes-1.18/

enabled.description: Enable ingress record generation for Invenio.

csrf_secret_salt.description: DEPRECATED: this is automatically generated now, or set by custom secret using invenio.existingSecret

dataCenterSymbol.description: DataCite data center symbol, it will translate into DATACITE_DATACENTER_SYMBOL

existingSecret.description: Existing secret name for datacite username and password

format.description: A string used for formatting the DOI, it will translate into DATACITE_FORMAT

prefix.description: DataCite DOI prefix, it will translate into DATACITE_PREFIX

passwordKey.description: Name of key in existing secret to use for password. Only used when `invenio.datacite.existingSecret` is set.

usernameKey.description: Name of key in existing secret to use for username. Only used when `invenio.datacite.existingSecret` is set.

testMode.description: DataCite test mode enabled, it will translate into DATACITE_TEST_MODE # This has to be a string value, Invenio will evaluate this as a python expression and transform it into a boolean. # Example: # testMode: "False"

default_users.description: Default users to create. Requires invenio.init=true

demo_data.description: Load demo data. Requires invenio.init=true and default_users to be set

existingSecret.description: General existing secret name for, at least, secret key and salts # If not set a new secret will be generated automatically with random values # ref: https://inveniordm.docs.cern.ch/customize/authentication/#security

existing_secret.description: DEPRECATED: this is automatically generated now, or set by custom secret using invenio.existingSecret

extraConfig.description: Extra environment variables (templated) to be added to all the pods

extraEnvFrom.description: Extra secretRef or configMapRef for the `envFrom` field in all Invenio containers (templated)

extraEnvVars.description: Extra environment variables to be added to all the pods

extraVolumeMounts.description: Extra volumeMounts for all Invenio containers

extraVolumes.description: Extra volumes for all Invenio pods

hostname.description: Invenio hostname (templated) used in configuration variables like TRUSTED_HOSTS, SITE_HOSTNAME or SITE_URL

init.description: Enable initial setup (create users, load demo data)

existing_secret.description: Use an existing secret for remote apps credentials

secret_name.description: Name of the existing secret for remote apps

secret_key.description: DEPRECATED: this is automatically generated now, or set by custom secret using invenio.existingSecret

security_login_salt.description: DEPRECATED: this is automatically generated now, or set by custom secret using invenio.existingSecret

dsn.description: Sentry DSN, required unless existingSecret is provided

existingSecret.description: Existing secret name for sentry's dsn

dsnKey.description: Name of key in existing secret to use for dsn

uwsgiExtraConfig.description: Extra configuration variables (key: value) to be added to the uWSGI configuration file

vocabularies.description: Vocabularies to be loaded as files under /app_data/vocabularies # Example # vocabularies: #   resource_types.yaml: | #    - id: publication #      icon: file alternate #      props: #        csl: report #        datacite_general: Text #        datacite_type: "" #        openaire_resourceType: "0017" #        openaire_type: publication #        eurepo: info:eu-repo/semantics/other #        schema.org: https://schema.org/CreativeWork #        subtype: "" #        type: publication #        marc21_type: publication #        marc21_subtype: "" #      title: #        en: Publication #      tags: #        - depositable #        - linkable

securityContext.description: SecurityContext for the kerberos container # ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-container

extraServerConfig.description: Extra configuration to be added to nginx.conf under the server section (templated)

client_max_body_size.description: Client max body size for files
Client max body size for records

securityContext.description: SecurityContext for the nginx container # ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-container

enabled.description: Switch to enable or disable the Opensearch helm chart

opensearchExternal.description: External Opensearch configuration # All of these values are only used when opensearch.enabled is set to false

storage_class.description: Storage class of backing PVC # If defined, storageClassName: <storageClass> # If set to "-", storageClassName: "", which disables dynamic provisioning # If undefined (the default) or set to null, no storageClassName spec is #   set, choosing the default provisioner.  (gp2 on AWS, standard on #   GKE, AWS & OpenStack)

useExistingClaim.description: Use an existing PVC to use for persistence

useSubPath.description: Enable or disable usage of subPath for PVC mounts # When enabled, each logical path is mounted using Kubernetes subPath, # providing directory-level isolation within a single PVC. # When disabled, the entire PVC is mounted at the base path and the # application is responsible for managing internal directory structure. # This option can improve compatibility with Kubernetes volume metrics # (e.g. kubelet_volume_stats_used_bytes) in some CSI environments such as Ceph CSI.

database.description: Name for a custom database to create

existingSecret.description: Name of the existing secret to get the password from

password.description: Password for the custom user to create

username.description: Name for a custom user to create

enabled.description: Switch to enable or disable the PostgreSQL helm chart

postgresqlExternal.description: External PostgreSQL configuration # All of these values are only used when postgresql.enabled is set to false

rabbitmqExternal.description: External RabbitMQ configuration # All of these values are only used when rabbitmq.enabled is set to false

redisExternal.description: External redis configuration # All of these values are only used when redis.enabled is set to false

annotations.description: Annotations to be added to the Route
Extra annotations for the terminal Deployment

labels.description: Extra labels for the terminal Deployment

spec.description: Configuration for other configurable fields in the Deployment.spec

extraEnvFrom.description: Extra secretRef or configMapRef for the `envFrom` field in the terminal container (templated)

extraEnvVars.description: Extra environment variables to be added to the terminal pods

extraVolumeMounts.description: Extra volumeMounts for the terminal container

extraVolumes.description: Extra volumes for the terminal Pod

securityContext.description: SecurityContext for the initContainers in the terminal pod # ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-container

nodeSelector.description: Node labels for terminal pods assignment # Ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/assign-pod-node/

podSecurityContext.description: SecurityContext for the terminal pod # ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-container

replicas.description: Number of replicas. Start with 0 to avoid resource usage

securityContext.description: SecurityContext for the terminal container # ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-container

tolerations.description: Tolerations for terminal pods assignment # Ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/taint-and-toleration/

affinity.description: Affinity rules for web pods assignment # Ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/assign-pod-node/#affinity-and-anti-affinity

readinessProbe.description: Templated `readinessProbe` for the web container # ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/configure-liveness-readiness-startup-probes/

replicas.description: Number of web replicas to deploy

securityContext.description: SecurityContext for the web container # ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-container

annotations.description: Add extra (templated) annotations to the web service

startupProbe.description: Templated `startupProbe` for the web container # ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/configure-liveness-readiness-startup-probes/

terminationGracePeriodSeconds.description: Seconds the pod needs to terminate gracefully # ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/pod/#termination-of-pods

tolerations.description: Tolerations for web pods assignment # Ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/taint-and-toleration/

processes.description: Number of uWSGI processes to run within each pod

threads.description: Number of uWSGI threads to run within each process

affinity.description: Affinity rules for worker pods assignment # Ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/assign-pod-node/#affinity-and-anti-affinity

app.description: Name of the celery app to run

concurrency.description: Number of concurrent tasks to run per worker
